narrative:
Skies are partly sunny today as our temperature rebound continues. Friday will be similarly mild, but there will be scattered afternoon showers and perhaps a thundershower. Temperatures take a very noticeable tumble for Friday night and Saturday; It will be a difference that you will really be able to feel, especially with the gusty winds. Then yet again this month, we begin the recovery on Sunday, en route to another mild week before Easter.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

More March Ups & Downs

Last Updated Thu March 26 at 10:29AM by Scott Stephens

Skies are partly sunny today as our temperature rebound continues. Friday will be similarly mild, but there will be scattered afternoon showers and perhaps a thundershower. Temperatures take a very noticeable tumble for Friday night and Saturday; It will be a difference that you will really be able to feel, especially with the gusty winds. Then yet again this month, we begin the recovery on Sunday, en route to another mild week before Easter.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

High pressure anchored offshore will direct mild, southwesterly breezes into the mountains for your Thursday with a mix of sun and clouds.

A strong, but somewhat moisture-deprived cold front, arrives Friday afternoon. There will be scattered showers and perhaps a thundershower during the afternoon and early evening before clearing takes place late Friday night.

A sharply colder air mass invades Friday night on gusty, northwest winds. This chill continues on Saturday with bright sunshine. After a second cold night Saturday night, Sunday will turn a bit milder by afternoon, but still a few degrees below normal.

Steady warming continues next week with above-average temperatures returning to the mountains.

With inherited drought from winter carrying over into spring, and in the absence of stronger weather systems, we will continue the dry theme through much of next week. A cold front might get close enough to spark some showers for the latter half of the workweek.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
